Semaphoresa
A garbage collected container of semaphores.
    This collection internally uses a weak value dictionary so that when a
    semaphore is no longer in use (by any threads) it will automatically be
    removed from this container by the garbage collector.
    c
weakref
WeakValueDictionary
_semaphores
	threading
Lock
_lock
self
./fsm/utils/lock_utils.py
__init__
Semaphores.__init__c
Gets (or creates) a semaphore with a given name.
        :param name: The semaphore name to get/create (used to associate
                     previously created names with the same semaphore).
        Returns an newly constructed semaphore (or an existing one if it was
        already created for the given name).
        N)
KeyErrorr
	Semaphore)

Semaphores.getc
z6Returns how many semaphores exist at the current time.)
lenr

Semaphores.__len__N)
__name__

__doc__r
_SEMAPHORESr
semaphoresr
internal_lock/
Context based lock
    This function yields a `threading.Semaphore` instance.
    :param do_log: Whether to log acquire/release messages.  This is primarily
      intended to reduce log message duplication when `lock` is used from the
      `synchronized` decorator.
    :param semaphores: Container that provides semaphores to use when locking.
        This ensures that threads inside the same application can not collide,
        due to the fact that external process locks are unaware of a processes
        active threads.
    )
Acquired semaphore "%(lock)s"
lockz
Releasing semaphore "%(lock)s"N)
debug)
do_logr
int_lockr
Synchronization decorator.
    Decorating a method like so::
        @synchronized('mylock')
        def foo(self, *args):
           ...
    ensures that only one thread will execute the foo method at a time.
    Different methods can share the same lock::
        @synchronized('mylock')
        def foo(self, *args):
           ...
        @synchronized('mylock')
        def bar(self, *args):
           ...
    This way only one of either foo or bar can be executing at a time.
    c
)	NF)
z;Lock "%(name)s" acquired by someonewaited %(wait_secs)0.3fs)
Z	wait_secsz
N/Az
%0.3fsz6Lock "%(name)s" released by someone held %(held_secs)s)

innerf
z)synchronized.<locals>.wrap.<locals>.inner)
	functools
wraps)
wrapd
synchronized.<locals>.wrapr
synchronizedL
RWLocka(
    Classic implementation of reader-writer lock with preference to writers.
    Readers can access a resource simultaneously.
    Writers get an exclusive access.
    API is self-descriptive:
        reader_enters()
        reader_leaves()
        writer_enters()
        writer_leaves()
    c
RLock
mutexr
can_read
	can_write
active_readers
active_writers
waiting_readers
